beats.activity: Wake up, coffee on the porch, check emails and garden status
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning ritual—Jackson starts quiet and observational, checking on his garden in good weather before the day unfolds. Low energy yesterday (45/100) means he's easing into the day at home.

beats.activity: Garden maintenance: watering, weeding, checking tomatoes and lettuce
beats.reasoning: Sunday is his day off from the shop. The sunny, calm weather is perfect for tending the garden—his favorite weekend activity. Yesterday he watered and pulled lettuce; today he'll maintain that routine.

beats.activity: Walk to local coffee shop, grab a pastry and espresso
beats.reasoning: A short neighborhood walk—Jackson is a homebody but likes his Sunday coffee ritual. Within walking distance in Northgate, gives him a small outing without leaving his comfort zone.

beats.activity: Return home, settle on porch with coffee and book; Spreadsheet work: review expenses from the week, organize bike maintenance logs
beats.reasoning: Back home to his porch—his sanctuary. Low energy day calls for quiet time reading, which fits his reflective personality and yesterday's mood.

beats.activity: Make lunch with fresh garden lettuce and tomatoes, eat on the porch
beats.reasoning: Lunch at home using his own produce—simple, satisfying, and reflects his self-sufficient style. Continues the porch-time theme.

beats.activity: Bike ride to Gas Works Park via Wallingford
beats.reasoning: A Sunday bike ride to a favorite spot—Jackson is a bike mechanic and weekend cyclist. The calm, sunny weather is perfect for a leisurely ride. He knows the route well from Day -3.

beats.activity: Sit at Gas Works Park, watch boat traffic on Lake Union, read and people-watch
beats.reasoning: Perfect Sunday afternoon—calm, sunny weather, recreational boat traffic on the lake. Jackson values quiet observation and time alone more than crowds, so he'll settle on the hill with his book and soak in the scene.

beats.activity: Bike ride home through Wallingford, stop for iced tea
beats.reasoning: Leisurely ride home in late afternoon. He'll grab an iced tea somewhere in Wallingford on the way—he did this on Day -3 and enjoyed it. Gives him a gentle transition back home.

beats.activity: Rest on porch, listen to music, light reading
beats.reasoning: Late afternoon wind-down at home—Jackson's happy place. Low energy day means he's staying close to home, enjoying quiet time with music and his book as evening approaches.

beats.activity: Prepare simple dinner with garden vegetables, eat on porch as light fades
beats.reasoning: Dinner using his own harvest—Jackson values self-sufficiency and simple, earned meals. Eating on the porch allows him to enjoy the calm Sunday evening.

beats.activity: Evening at home: review expenses, listen to music, read in the living room
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening at home before bed. Jackson spends time on his spreadsheets (as he did yesterday), music, and reading—his favorite Sunday-night activities. Keeps him at home for a smooth transition to sleep.
Full night's rest at home after a quiet, restorative Sunday. Seven and a half hours of sleep to recover for the work week ahead.

diary: Sunday felt like a gift—one of those days where everything just slots into place without trying. Started with coffee on the porch in that perfect early light, watching the garden come alive. The tomatoes are finally looking good; I actually used some in lunch, which felt like a small victory. Rode out to Gas Works around midday and found the whole lake buzzing with boats and paddleboarders. I sat there for over an hour with my book, just letting the sun warm my face and watching the chaos. The ride home through Wallingford was smooth, grabbed an iced tea at that little spot I like. Spent the rest of the evening drifting between the porch and inside—music, reading, simple dinner with more garden greens. By the time dark fell, I felt genuinely settled. No rush, no noise. Just the kind of Sunday that reminds you why you stay put.
thought_process: The weather pulled me outside early and kept me there. With no major city drama today, I leaned into routine—the garden work and spreadsheets grounded me, gave structure to the morning. But honestly, I was craving movement and openness, so the bike ride to Gas Works felt essential. Watching all that recreational energy on the lake while staying still myself was oddly restorative. I think at 52, I'm learning that the best Sundays aren't packed; they're paced. The fresh lettuce and tomatoes for lunch weren't just meals—they were proof that patience pays off. The evening unwound naturally; I wasn't chasing anything, just letting the day fade into rest. Calm weather, a quiet city, my own space—that's the sweet spot.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
